name: Computational Complexity: Big O Notation Visualizer
description:
The Hierarchy of Efficiency

Big O notation is a mathematical notation that describes the limiting behavior of a function when the argument tends towards a particular value or infinity. In computer science, it is used to classify algorithms according to how their run time or space requirements grow as the input size (n) increases.

  • O(1) - Constant: Execution time is independent of input size (e.g., accessing an array index).
  • O(log n) - Logarithmic: Very efficient. Doubling the input only adds one step (e.g., Binary Search).
  • O(n) - Linear: Time grows proportionally to input size (e.g., finding the max value in an unsorted list).
  • O(n log n) - Linearithmic: Standard for efficient sorting algorithms like Merge Sort or Quick Sort.
  • O(n²) - Quadratic: Efficiency decreases rapidly. Typical for nested loops (e.g., Bubble Sort).
  • O(2ⁿ) - Exponential: Computational cost doubles with every addition to input (e.g., recursive Fibonacci).
  • O(n!) - Factorial: Explosive growth. Becomes impractical for almost any $n > 10$ (e.g., Traveling Salesperson brute force).

The chart is clipped at 500 operations to demonstrate how quickly non-linear algorithms become unfeasible compared to logarithmic or linear alternatives.
dataGenerationCode: const nArr = []; const o1 = [], oLog = [], oN = [], oNLog = [], oN2 = [], o2N = [], oFact = []; const fact = (num) => { let r = 1; for (let i = 2; i <= num; i++) r *= i; return r; }; for (let i = 1; i <= 50; i++) { nArr.push(i); o1.push(1); oLog.push(Math.log2(i)); oN.push(i); oNLog.push(i * Math.log2(i)); oN2.push(i * i); o2N.push(Math.pow(2, i)); oFact.push(i > 12 ? null : fact(i)); } return [ { x: nArr, y: o1, name: 'O(1)', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#94a3b8', width: 3, shape: 'spline' } }, { x: nArr, y: oLog, name: 'O(log n)', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#3b82f6', width: 3, shape: 'spline' } }, { x: nArr, y: oN, name: 'O(n)', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#10b981', width: 3, shape: 'spline' } }, { x: nArr, y: oNLog, name: 'O(n log n)', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#f59e0b', width: 3, shape: 'spline' } }, { x: nArr, y: oN2, name: 'O(n²)', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#f97316', width: 3, shape: 'spline' } }, { x: nArr, y: o2N, name: 'O(2ⁿ)', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#ef4444', width: 3, shape: 'spline' } }, { x: nArr, y: oFact, name: 'O(n!)', type: 'scatter', mode: 'lines', line: { color: '#ec4899', width: 3, shape: 'spline' } } ];
